answer:
To close an MCP server session, the connection is terminated during the shutdown phase of the protocol lifecycle [1]. No specific shutdown message exists in the protocol; instead, the connection is closed using the underlying transport mechanism [1].

For Stdio transport, the client initiates shutdown by closing the input stream to the server process [1]. If the server does not exit, the client should send a SIGTERM and subsequently a SIGKILL [1]. For HTTP transports, shutdown is indicated by closing the associated HTTP connections [1]. When using the Go SDK, you can call the Close method on the client session [2].

Building MCP servers for ChatGPT Apps and API integrations
